query_normalized: the influence of algorithmic recommendations on exploration vs. exploitation in online learning psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on the engineering architecture and performance metrics (retention, engagement) of a specific fashion recommender system, rather than investigating the psychological mechanisms of "exploration vs. exploitation" or the behavioral influence of algorithms on user learning. It falls under the "off-domain entirely" rejection rule because it addresses system optimization in e-commerce rather than the cognitive or behavioral science constructs central to the user's question.
